response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior engineer with strong fundamentals (TDD, distributed systems exposure, Linux, low-latency work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) and some geospatial background via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role specifically requires modern C++ or Rust as primary languages, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure a decade ago at National Instruments, with no Rust. Core experience is in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Could be considered if the team is open to a strong generalist willing to ramp on C++, but is not a direct technical match.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput backend work (Sociomantic) and distributed systems exposure
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing culture
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience (only ~6 months at NI in 2014-2015) and no Rust
Primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the role's required stack
No demonstrated experience building production routing/navigation tiling pipelines

key_factors.factor: Senior engineering rigor and low-latency systems experience
